title: prangos pabularia subsp. cylindrocarpa
intro: Prangos pabularia subsp. cylindrocarpa, also known as Ferula cypraea, is a plant species in the carrot family, native to Iran and Afghanistan. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic effects, making it a potential treatment for wounds, infections, and pain relief. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, prevent scarring, and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant's rhizome is used to make herbal preparations, such as an oil or an extract, which can be applied topically to the affected area.
